title: arXiv Query: search_query=&id_list=2309.15654&start=0&max_results=1
summary: Valued constraint satisfaction problems (VCSPs) constitute a large class of computational optimization problems. It was shown recently that, over finite domains, every VCSP is in P or NP-complete, depending on the admitted cost functions. In this article, we study cost functions over countably infinite domains whose automorphisms form an oligomorphic permutation group. Our results include a hardness condition based on a generalization of pp-constructability as known from classical CSPs and a pol
